pairs.question: Which ships participated in John Franklin's lost expedition in 1845?
pairs.answer: HMS Terror and HMS Erebus participated in John Franklin's lost expedition in 1845.

pairs.question: What were the original and converted purposes of HMS Terror (1813)?
pairs.answer: HMS Terror (1813) was originally launched as a 10-gun bomb vessel in 1813 and was later converted to a discovery vessel in 1836.

pairs.question: How many Royal Navy ships have borne the name HMS Terror, and which one was involved in the bombardment of Fort McHenry?
pairs.answer: Nine Royal Navy ships have borne the name HMS Terror. The HMS Terror (1813) was involved in the bombardment of Fort McHenry during the War of 1812.

pairs.question: Describe the final fate of HMS Terror (1759).
pairs.answer: HMS Terror (1759) was an 8-gun bomb ketch sold in 1774, renamed Union, and later lost on 20 May 1782 off the Malabar coast of India.

pairs.question: What was the role of HMS Terror (1856) after its launch?
pairs.answer: HMS Terror (1856) was launched as a 16-gun iron screw floating battery, became a base ship at Bermuda in 1857, and was sold in 1902.

pairs.question: Why were HMS Terror (1813) and HMS Erebus converted to screw propulsion in 1844?
pairs.answer: HMS Terror (1813) and HMS Erebus were converted to screw propulsion in 1844 in preparation for their second polar expedition, which aimed to explore the Arctic regions.
